sec:1:12.summary: Prime $k$-tuples conjecture: every admissible $\mathcal{H} = \{h_1,\ldots,h_k\}$ produces infinitely many $n$ with all $n+h_i$ prime. The motivating conjecture; the paper proves an unconditional weaker form via a stronger sieve.

thm:1.1.summary: For every $m$, $\liminf_n (p_{n+m} - p_n) \ll m^3 e^{4m}$. Bounded $m$-th prime gaps for all $m$ — qualitatively new beyond GPY/Zhang.

thm:1.2.summary: Positive proportion of $m$-element subsets of any large finite set of integers are admissible $m$-tuples that capture infinitely many simultaneous primes.

thm:1.3.summary: Unconditionally, $\liminf_n (p_{n+1} - p_n) \le 600$. The headline bounded-gaps result, sharper than Zhang ($\le 7\times 10^7$) and Polymath8a ($\le 4680$).

thm:1.4.summary: Under generalized Elliott-Halberstam (level of distribution $\theta$ for every $\theta < 1$): $\liminf_n (p_{n+1} - p_n) \le 12$ and $\liminf_n (p_{n+2} - p_n) \le 600$.

prop:4.1.summary: Sieve sum asymptotics. For sieve weights $\lambda$ defined from a smooth $F$ supported on the simplex $\mathcal{R}_k$, $S_1$ and $S_2$ are computed in terms of integrals $I_k(F)$ and $J_k^{(m)}(F)$. The technical heart of the new $k$-dimensional Selberg sieve.

prop:4.2.summary: Sieve-to-primes bridge. If primes have level of distribution $\theta$, then for $r_k = \lceil \theta M_k/2 \rceil$ where $M_k = \sup_F \big(\sum_m J_k^{(m)}/I_k\big)$, there are infinitely many $n$ with $r_k$ of the $n+h_i$ prime. Reduces gap bounds to a variational problem.

prop:4.3.summary: Explicit bounds on $M_k$: $M_5 > 2$ (gives thm:1.4 conditional), $M_{105} > 4$ (gives thm:1.3 unconditional), and $M_k > \log k - 2\log\log k - 2$ for large $k$ (gives thm:1.1 and thm:1.2).

lem:5.1.summary: Change of variables: rewrites $S_1$ in diagonalized form using $y_{r_1,\ldots,r_k} = \big(\prod_i \mu(r_i)\varphi(r_i)\big) \sum_d \lambda_d/\prod_i d_i$, with controlled error term.

lem:5.2.summary: Diagonalizes $S_2^{(m)}$ (the $m$-th term of $S_2$) in terms of analogous $y^{(m)}$ variables, using the multiplicative function $g(p) = p-2$ from prime $k$-tuple singular series.

lem:5.3.summary: Linking lemma: relates $y^{(m)}$ to $y$ when $r_m = 1$, giving $y^{(m)} = \sum_{a_m} y_{\ldots,a_m,\ldots}/\varphi(a_m)$ plus error. The bridge that lets one optimize over a single function $F$.

lem:6.1.summary: Selberg-type weighted sum identity: $\sum_{d<z} \mu(d)^2 g(d) G(\log d/\log z) = \mathfrak{S} \log z \int_0^1 G + O(\mathfrak{S} L G_{\max})$. The fundamental analytic estimate underlying the smooth choice of weights.

lem:6.2.summary: Smooth-$F$ evaluation of $S_1 = (\varphi(W)^k N (\log R)^k / W^{k+1}) I_k(F) + \text{error}$. Together with lem:6.3 this proves prop:4.1.

lem:6.3.summary: Smooth-$F$ evaluation of $S_2^{(m)}$ in terms of $J_k^{(m)}(F)$. Companion to lem:6.2; together they establish prop:4.1.

lem:8.1.summary: Closed-form simplex moment integrals: $\int_{\mathcal{R}_k} (1-P_1)^a P_j^b\,dt = (a!/(k+jb+a)!)\,G_{b,j}(k)$, where $G_{b,j}$ is an explicit polynomial in $k$. Foundation for explicit small-$k$ computations.

lem:8.2.summary: When $F$ is built from a polynomial $P$ in symmetric power sums $P_1, P_2$, both $I_k(F)$ and $J_k^{(m)}(F)$ reduce to explicit finite linear combinations of values of $G$ from lem:8.1. Turns the variational problem into linear algebra.

lem:8.3.summary: Generalized Rayleigh quotient: $\mathbf{a}^T A_2 \mathbf{a} / \mathbf{a}^T A_1 \mathbf{a}$ is maximized at the top eigenvector of $A_1^{-1}A_2$, with value the top eigenvalue. Reduces $M_k$ lower bounds for $k=5, 105$ to a finite eigenvalue computation.

edges.note: Bounded $m$-th gaps follow from prop:4.2 applied with $r_k \ge m+1$; combined with the lower bound on $M_k$ (prop:4.3 part 3) gives the $m^3 e^{4m}$ estimate.
Uses $M_k > \log k - 2\log\log k - 2$ (part 3) to bound the required $k$ for $r_k = m+1$ primes.
Reduces a positive fraction of $m$-tuples problem to existence of admissible $\mathcal{H}$ giving $r_k \ge m$ primes via prop:4.2.
Uses asymptotic $M_k > \log k$ bound (part 3).
Apply with $k = 105$ and $\theta = 1/2$ (Bombieri-Vinogradov): $r_{105} \ge 2$; min admissible 105-tuple has diameter 600.
Under GEH $\theta \to 1$, $k = 5$: $r_5 \ge 2 \Rightarrow \liminf_n (p_{n+1}-p_n) \le 12$ (diameter of admissible 5-tuple).
Uses $M_5 > 2$ (part 1).
Combines $S_2 - \rho S_1 > 0$ using the asymptotics of prop:4.1 to extract primes.
prop:4.2 is the unconditional partial result toward the prime $k$-tuples conjecture: $r_k$ of $k$ primes infinitely often.
$S_1$ evaluation comes from lem:6.2.
$S_2^{(m)}$ evaluation comes from lem:6.3.
Diagonalized form of $S_1$ in $y$-variables.
Selberg-type weighted sum identity bounds the $y$-side sums when $y$ comes from smooth $F$.
Diagonalized form of $S_2^{(m)}$.
Bridges $y^{(m)}$ back to $y$ derived from $F$.
Selberg-type identity again drives the asymptotic.
Parts 1 ($M_5 > 2$) and 2 ($M_{105} > 4$) are proved by exhibiting explicit polynomial $F$ and evaluating $I_k, J_k^{(m)}$ via lem:8.2.
Top of the ratio $\big(\sum_m J_k^{(m)}\big)/I_k(F)$ is a generalized eigenvalue; computed via lem:8.3 over polynomial ansatz.
Explicit simplex moment integrals feed lem:8.2's reduction.
Each $(1-P_1)^b P_2^c$ moment evaluated via lem:8.1.
thm:1.3 is the $m=1$ case with explicit constant 600; thm:1.1 is the qualitative version for all $m$.

main_results.description: The four headline statements: bounded gaps (thm:1.3), conditional improvement (thm:1.4), bounded $m$-th gaps (thm:1.1), and density of admissible $m$-tuples (thm:1.2).

k_dimensional_sieve_framework.description: The new multidimensional Selberg sieve: weights are squared sums over $k$-tuples of divisors with a smooth shape function $F$ on the simplex $\mathcal{R}_k$. prop:4.1 estimates $S_1, S_2$; prop:4.2 turns the ratio into $r_k$ primes; prop:4.3 supplies the explicit/asymptotic $M_k$.

diagonalization_layer.description: Selberg-style change of variables from $\lambda$ to $y$ (and $y^{(m)}$) that makes the sieve sums tractable.

smooth_weight_layer.description: Analytic estimates that evaluate $S_1, S_2$ to leading order when $F$ is smooth, proving prop:4.1.

explicit_optimization_layer.description: Closed-form moment computations and a generalized eigenvalue argument that supply the explicit $M_5 > 2$ and $M_{105} > 4$ used for thm:1.3 and thm:1.4.

GPY.title: Primes in tuples. I
GPY.role: foundational — direct ancestor; one-dimensional Selberg sieve approach Maynard generalizes

GoldstonYildirim.title: Higher correlations of divisor sums related to primes. III. Small gaps between primes

GPY:ManyPrimes.title: Primes in tuples. III. On the difference $p_{n+\nu} - p_n$

GGPY.title: Small gaps between products of two primes

Zhang.title: Bounded gaps between primes
Zhang.role: parallel breakthrough — first bounded-gap proof ($\le 7\times 10^7$); independent route via expanded Bombieri-Vinogradov beyond $1/2$

Polymath.title: A new bound for gaps between primes (Polymath8a)
Polymath.role: parallel — Polymath8a optimization of Zhang's method down to 4680

ElliottHalberstam.title: A conjecture in prime number theory
ElliottHalberstam.role: foundational — EH conjecture; thm:1.4 uses generalized form

FriedlanderGranville.title: Limitations to the equi-distribution of primes. I
